GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "All aboard: 6 reasons why you should consider a Cunard voyage for your next holiday", "Why you should consider luxury cruise line Cunard for your next holiday.", "A trip at sea has historically been a study in indulgent luxury. Who could forget those iconic images of the ever-stylish Elizabeth Taylor on-board the Queen Mary for her 14-week honeymoon, or those classic black and white images of cinema heroine Grace Kelly arriving in Monaco to wed her prince? A world voyage has always been steeped in style and British heritage brand Cunard continues to honour this, leading the way with a luxurious fleet of ships that are certainly bucket-list worthy. But don't wait – consider booking your own world voyage today – here are six reasons why you should consider a Cunard voyage for your next holiday. You can go anywhere around the world. Cunard's sumptuous ships journey around the globe. From Cape Town to Dubai or even a jaunt to NYC there is a port of call for everyone, regardless of your interests. Cunard can also open your eyes to Australia's beauty, with ships travelling around the country too. What better way to see our country than from the sea? It features luxury accommodation. The Staterooms and Suites on board a Cunard ship are world class. Cunard's focus is on comfort and elegance, with all Staterooms and Suites offering a spacious oasis which will make you feel at home, from the grand Queens Grill Suite to the Britannia stateroom with your own private balcony. The dining is five star. Toast the culinary highlights on board. One of the most incredible parts of experiencing a Cunard voyage is the dining experience aboard Cunard's fleet. Chefs aboard the Cunard fleet, such as Mark Oldroyd, executive chef of Queen Elizabeth, serve up daily gastronomic delights to complement the location in which each ship docks. For instance, consider dining on dishes like tiger prawns, pickled ginger, brown butter and crispy curry leaves, before visiting the on-board specialty champagne lounge, fitted out with bubbles courtesy of Laurent Perrier. It's the perfect excuse to embrace resort attire. Create your own cruise collection with outfits and accessories befitting your luxurious surrounds because on board a Cunard ship, dressing for the occasion is half the fun. Then there's the shopping! Luxury labels available to shop on board include Max Mara, Mulberry, Kate Spade and Furla; but if that isn't enough, you can revel in the glamour, dressing up and fashionable highlights of Transatlantic Fashion Week. Previous designers showing have included Julian McDonald, Stuart Weitzman and Stephen Jones and in 2020 Cunard will welcome Badgley Mischka. You can rejuvenate at the on-board day spa. Whether for indulgence or upkeep, Cunard's spa menu offers a slice of wellness at sea. While aboard a Cunard cruise on Queen Elizabeth or Queen Victoria, there is an extensive menu for the Mareel Wellness & Beauty spas. Many of the wellness rituals take inspiration from the surrounding ocean, with the spas' signature treatment – Ocean Discovery – even incorporating marine-derived elixirs. While on board, also consider a massage or yoga session, while if it's some upkeep you're after, you can diarise blow-dries at the salon (which is stocked with KГ©rastase expert hair-care products), as well as waxing and nail appointments with highly trained attendants. It's seriously fun. What's a holiday if not a chance to kick up your heels and enjoy yourself, too? During a Cunard voyage, an evening itinerary could see you dancing at a gala, catching the latest Oscar-nominated film inside the luxurious on-board cinema, watching a theatre show, chuckling at live comedy, or enjoying a concert or recital. After all, on a Cunard ship, the journey is all yours."
